jokes.text: If she doesn't scream soon, I'm gonna have to give my frog a drink. Ferdinand gets dehydrated.
jokes.setup: Waiting for sister to find frog in bed
jokes.punchline: Caring about frog's hydration needs during prank
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdist logic - Will caring about the frog's welfare mid-prank shows his personality

jokes.text: We're going to bed just as soon as we finish this frog-leg soup.
jokes.setup: Kids scared about their frog Ferdinand
jokes.punchline: Adults eating frog soup, implying they caught and cooked Ferdinand
jokes.explanation: Dark but family-friendly subversion with excellent economy - the misdirection lands perfectly

jokes.text: There wasn't enough frog to make more than two bowls.
jokes.setup: Kids asking for some frog soup
jokes.punchline: Doubling down that they actually ate the whole frog
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the frog soup bit, though less surprising than the initial reveal

jokes.text: Ferdinand? This is a guy frog?
jokes.setup: Learning the frog's name
jokes.punchline: Kid focused on frog's gender rather than it being eaten
jokes.explanation: Good child logic - missing the point entirely and focusing on irrelevant detail

jokes.text: That's funny, I thought she said she put snakes in your sleeping bags. Psych!
jokes.setup: Casual mention of snakes in sleeping bags
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was another fake-out prank
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection with period-appropriate 'Psych!' delivery

jokes.text: That means 'just kidding.'
jokes.setup: Using slang term 'psych'
jokes.punchline: Explaining slang to confused kids
jokes.explanation: Mild generational humor, shows Will as cultural translator

jokes.text: Wait. Hold up. Scream for me one time.
jokes.setup: About to tell scary story
jokes.punchline: Testing their scream quality first like a performance coach
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Will's showman personality - treating storytelling like a performance

jokes.text: Any of you have pacemakers on?
jokes.setup: Warning story is frightening
jokes.punchline: Asking kids if they have medical devices
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - treating kids like elderly audience members

jokes.text: It happened to a guy named Will... bert. Wilbert Smith... sonian.
jokes.setup: Telling story about someone else
jokes.punchline: Obviously making up name on the fly from his own name
jokes.explanation: Classic transparent attempt at disguising autobiography as fiction, well-executed with the stammering

jokes.text: It was graffiti everywhere, even on the principal.
jokes.setup: School was rough with graffiti everywhere
jokes.punchline: Even on the human principal
jokes.explanation: Great visual absurdism with perfect economy - escalates the 'tough school' concept to ridiculous extreme

jokes.text: Bankster! I'd like you to meet my cousin... Willster!
jokes.setup: Carlton introducing Will to his prep school friends
jokes.punchline: Adding '-ster' suffix to everyone's name in affected prep style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character comedy - his affected prep school mannerisms on full display

jokes.text: Way to play. I kid you not, Will, women faint at this man's feet. Odor-Eaters work for me.
jokes.setup: Chad bragging about women fainting at his feet
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan response about foot odor products
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and deflation - Will's street-smart pragmatism cuts through prep school bragging perfectly

jokes.text: Outrageous. This guy's seriously twisted. He gets in a good one now and then.
jokes.setup: Will's deadpan comeback
jokes.punchline: Prep boys impressed by basic joke, Carlton's backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing prep boys' low humor standards and Carlton's condescension

jokes.text: I took the Universal Tour. You know that earthquake ride? Look out! Here comes the subway car!
jokes.setup: Simon offering to give school tour
jokes.punchline: Will referencing Universal Studios tour with physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's different cultural references, physical comedy implied but relies on pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: Excuse my cousin. He just doesn't know.
jokes.setup: Will making Universal Studios reference
jokes.punchline: Carlton's embarrassed apology for Will's behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton - patronizing and embarrassed by Will's different cultural background

jokes.text: Y'all don't throw nothing away.
jokes.setup: Seeing old championship oars from 1957
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact observation about rich people keeping everything
jokes.explanation: Good class-conscious observation delivered in Will's authentic voice

jokes.text: Shaker Heights, Ohio. But when I was doing research... on my main man Langston Hughes, I lived in Harlem. And believe me, those two weeks changed my life.
jokes.setup: White teacher claiming to understand Will's background
jokes.punchline: Revealing he lived in Harlem for only two weeks as 'research'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of well-meaning but clueless liberal educators - the timing and specificity make it land perfectly

jokes.text: I can tell.
jokes.setup: Teacher quoting Langston Hughes poetry dramatically
jokes.punchline: Will's flat, unimpressed response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation with maximum economy - two words destroy the teacher's pretentious moment

jokes.text: Anytime you feel like grooving, come over and get down with my bad self.
jokes.setup: White teacher trying to connect with Will
jokes.punchline: Using outdated/inappropriate slang awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - white teacher appropriating slang badly, perfectly executed character work

jokes.text: Where are all the fly honeys at? - Fly honeys? - Yeah, girls. Will, didn't anybody tell you this is an all-boys school?
jokes.setup: Will asking about attractive girls at school
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's an all-boys school
jokes.explanation: Solid setup and payoff showing Will's priorities and the culture clash

jokes.text: Then they tortured him with a half an hour of Broadway show tunes.
jokes.setup: Describing the school trying to break him down
jokes.punchline: Treating show tunes as actual torture method
jokes.explanation: Great cultural perspective joke - what prep schools see as enriching culture, Will sees as punishment

jokes.text: Enter the Bloat. Five, four, three, two...
jokes.setup: Introducing the evil teacher in his story
jokes.punchline: Counting down like a TV director before teacher's entrance
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's TV/entertainment background bleeding into his storytelling style

jokes.text: I am Dr... B, period, Langford... Oates.
jokes.setup: Teacher making dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, pretentious way of stating his name with pauses
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - pompous academic speaking style perfectly captured

jokes.text: Yo! - Mr. Svenson? - Ja?
jokes.setup: Will responding to roll call with 'Yo!'
jokes.punchline: Teacher thinks he's calling on Swedish student Svenson
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural miscommunication - Will's street greeting misunderstood as calling on foreign student

jokes.text: Is that like a trick question?
jokes.setup: Teacher asking if Will sees difference between himself and other students
jokes.punchline: Will's innocent response to obviously loaded question
jokes.explanation: Good tension diffusion - Will's genuine confusion at the racist undertones

jokes.text: You mean Section 3, Paragraph 4, which states: 'A tie must be worn with a Windsor knot.' But it doesn't say where.
jokes.setup: Teacher citing dress code about neckties
jokes.punchline: Will finding loophole - doesn't specify WHERE tie must be worn
jokes.explanation: Excellent rule-lawyering comedy - Will using system against itself with perfect logic and timing

jokes.text: Weren't you the bad guy in Goldfinger?
jokes.setup: Teacher mentioning Thomas Paine
jokes.punchline: Will confusing him with Bond villain Oddjob actor
jokes.explanation: Pop culture confusion showing Will's different cultural frame of reference from academic setting

jokes.text: Smith, ho!
jokes.setup: Prep school kid trying to greet Will street-style
jokes.punchline: Using completely wrong slang
jokes.explanation: Good setup for cultural education moment. The wrongness is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Listen, Cornflake.
jokes.setup: Student named Kellogg Lieberbaum trying to use street slang
jokes.punchline: Will calling him Cornflake (breakfast cereal reference)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting Kellogg's name to cereal brand with perfect economy

jokes.text: 'Ho' is definitely not a word that you want to yell. I think the word you're looking for is 'yo.' 'Yo'? Yeah. It'll be easy to remember. It's like 'oy' backwards.
jokes.setup: Rich Jewish kid yelling 'ho' inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Will explaining 'yo' as 'oy' backwards - connecting to Jewish culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural bridge-building - Will connecting street slang to Jewish expressions to help rich kid understand

jokes.text: Carlton, here in Boys Town... I don't want to rub anybody any way.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will is 'rubbing people the wrong way'
jokes.punchline: Will's suggestive response about not wanting to rub anyone at all-boys school
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning Carlton's phrase sexual in context of all-boys school

jokes.text: That's not funny. Nothing you say is funny.
jokes.setup: Will making sexual wordplay joke
jokes.punchline: Carlton's frustrated denial that ironically affirms the comedy
jokes.explanation: Classic straight-man frustration that becomes funny through Carlton's earnestness

jokes.text: The people who count were laughing at you, not with you.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he was funny in class
jokes.punchline: Carlton's harsh reality check about social hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Cutting line that establishes the prep school's cruel social dynamics

jokes.text: What, did you like pass out in history class? I had them rolling. The people who count were laughing at you, not with you.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he was funny in class
jokes.punchline: Carlton's cutting distinction between laughing with vs. at
jokes.explanation: Sharp character work - Carlton's elitist perspective cutting through Will's confidence

jokes.text: Then by all means, go. Might I recommend the gourmet deli on Roxbury?
jokes.setup: Will saying he could go for some pâté
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggesting Will go get it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - politely telling Will to serve himself

jokes.text: When I said I could go for it, I mean, like, I want it... like you go for it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey misunderstanding Will's request
jokes.punchline: Will awkwardly explaining he wants to be served
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's discomfort with having servants and class dynamics

jokes.text: They think it was a freshman who got halfway through and lost his cool. He lost his cool? That's funny. 'Fresh' means cool, right, Will?
jokes.setup: Theory that freshman carved 'fresh' but didn't finish 'freshman'
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv noting irony that 'fresh' means cool and Will is the Fresh Prince
jokes.explanation: Meta humor connecting show title to plot - fresh means cool but also references Will's persona

jokes.text: Day damn one, Vivian. Champagne. Cork it.
jokes.setup: Phil celebrating Will's success then learning about vandalism
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal from celebration to frustration, telling Geoffrey to cork the champagne
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character - Phil's instant mood swing with great physical comedy implied

jokes.text: All I can say to you, Will, is tsk squared.
jokes.setup: Phil expressing disappointment in Will
jokes.punchline: Mathematical expression for double disapproval
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil humor - intellectual/mathematical spin on common expression of disapproval

jokes.text: There's such a thing as dog dumb, too.
jokes.setup: Carlton lecturing about honor code
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart retort using parallel structure
jokes.explanation: Great comeback using Carlton's own structure against him - Will's street wisdom vs prep school rules

jokes.text: Maybe on my deathbed.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking if Will intends to confess
jokes.punchline: Extreme time frame for potential confession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's stubbornness with economical delivery

jokes.text: And not, as Mr. Smith contends... the manufacturer of Ring Dings.
jokes.setup: Teaching about Dolley Madison in American history
jokes.punchline: Referencing Will's earlier confusion of historical figure with snack cake brand
jokes.explanation: Solid callback showing ongoing conflict and Will's cultural references

jokes.text: He did it.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking for confession about vandalism
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately tattling on Will
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton betrayal - maximum impact with minimum words, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Gentlemen, my cousin has committed a vicious crime. He could not be guiltier.
jokes.setup: Carlton supposed to defend Will
jokes.punchline: Immediately throwing Will under the bus instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Carlton's complete failure to defend Will while claiming to help

jokes.text: Yeah. That's what I would have said.
jokes.setup: Carlton completely condemning Will while claiming to help
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response to the 'defense'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Carlton's betrayal - sarcasm delivered with resignation

jokes.text: No, not the Alligaroos.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting Will be banned from the Alligaroos
jokes.punchline: Will's fake devastation at losing something he doesn't want
jokes.explanation: Good mock-horror at losing membership to group he clearly despises

jokes.text: Speak on, bro.
jokes.setup: Teacher making condescending speech about Will
jokes.punchline: Will encouraging his own roasting
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's resilience and street smarts. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Where he grew up, the word 'bad' means 'good.' Doesn't that say it all?
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to defend Will with cultural sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Revealing his own prejudice while claiming to help
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of condescending liberal attitudes - teacher reveals his prejudice while trying to be helpful

jokes.text: But those guys, they carved their name in a new desk. They got their picture on the wall. That's totally different. They carved their names so people would remember them as part of the school. That's why I did it.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his desk carving was vandalism
jokes.punchline: Pointing out hypocrisy - founders did same thing but are honored for it
jokes.explanation: Excellent logic comedy exposing institutional hypocrisy - Will's most articulate moment shows character growth

jokes.text: Word up, Cornflake.
jokes.setup: Rich kid supporting Will
jokes.punchline: Using Will's nickname for him while adopting his slang
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback showing Will's cultural influence and the kid embracing his nickname

jokes.text: I'm taking my dad's checkbook with me.
jokes.setup: Rich kid threatening to leave school
jokes.punchline: The real threat - taking away daddy's money
jokes.explanation: Perfect private school economics - money talks louder than principles

jokes.text: Just stand there doing nothing... saying nothing... arms at your side like a zombie. Breathing just enough to keep the blood circulating. And no more.
jokes.setup: Phil giving Will advice about not causing trouble
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific instructions reducing Will to minimal life functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Phil's exasperation - reducing Will to zombie-like minimal existence

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Uncle Phil. I wasn't listening. What did you say?
jokes.setup: Phil's detailed instructions for Will to do nothing
jokes.punchline: Will proving he can't follow even the simplest instruction by not listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - Will immediately demonstrates why Phil's fears are justified

jokes.text: Good night, Will. Lock your door when you go to bed.
jokes.setup: Phil frustrated with Will not listening
jokes.punchline: Ominous suggestion Will should protect himself from Phil's anger
jokes.explanation: Dark but family-friendly threat implying Phil might harm Will - good character moment

jokes.text: Carlton, you Five-Oed me, man. - Excuse me? - You dimed me out. - Pardon? - You tattled on me, all right?
jokes.setup: Will trying to confront Carlton
jokes.punchline: Having to translate street slang multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect cultural communication gap. The escalating translation is great character work.

jokes.text: If I woke up one morning and I had all of your friends... and I was an Alligaroo... and I wore those cute shirts with the little horses on them... I'd jump off the Empire State Building and hope to catch my eyelid on a nail.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Will should want to fit in with his friends
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and graphic description of preferring death to prep school life
jokes.explanation: Exceptional escalation and imagery - the 'eyelid on a nail' detail is grotesque perfection, perfectly captures Will's contempt

jokes.text: With quips like that, it's no wonder you're the toast of the town.
jokes.setup: Will's dramatic speech about preferring death to prep school life
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic response about Will's popularity
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton comeback showing his pretentious vocabulary and sarcasm

jokes.text: Picture this if you will... my man Wilbert in a kitchen with a hair net on his head. If that's not a scary thought, I don't know what is.
jokes.setup: Ending scary story about Wilbert/Will
jokes.punchline: The 'horror' is Will in kitchen duty with hair net
jokes.explanation: Good callback to framing device with visual comedy of Will's punishment

jokes.text: Will's cold busted.
jokes.setup: Will making kids promise not to tell Wilbert Smithsonian
jokes.punchline: Kids revealing they know Will IS Wilbert
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to framing device - kids weren't fooled by Will's thin disguise
